heading: State cooperation, aid, and training
label: 136u State cooperation, aid, and training
notes: Amendments1978—Subsec. (a). extended provisions to Indian tribes, authorized annual appropriation of funds for training and certification programs, and required proportionate reduction of shares in the allocation of available funds when appropriations do not cover 50 percent of the annual costs.Pub. L. 95–396Subsec. (b). authorized contracts with Indian tribal agencies.Pub. L. 95–396Subsec. (c). substituted “shall” for “may”, substituted “use” for “utilize”, and “to inform and educate pesticide users about accepted uses and other regulations” for “for informing farmers of accepted uses and other regulations”.Pub. L. 95–396

L. 105–276, title III112 Stat. 2499Oct. 21, 1998“That beginning in fiscal year 1999 and thereafter, pesticide program implementation grants under section 23(a)(1) of the Federal Insecticide, Fungicide and Rodenticide Act, as amended [], shall be available for pesticide program development and implementation, including enforcement and compliance activities”.7 U.S.C. 136u(a)(1)

children.text: to delegate to any State or Indian tribe the authority to cooperate in the enforcement of this subchapter through the use of its personnel or facilities, to train personnel of the State or Indian tribe to cooperate in the enforcement of this subchapter, and to assist States and Indian tribes in implementing cooperative enforcement programs through grants-in-aid; and

to assist States in developing and administering State programs, and Indian tribes that enter into cooperative agreements, to train and certify applicators consistent with the standards the Administrator prescribes.

children.heading: Contracts for training
children.label: b Contracts for training
children.text: In addition, the Administrator may enter into contracts with Federal, State, or Indian tribal agencies for the purpose of encouraging the training of certified applicators.

children.heading: Information and education
children.label: c Information and education
children.text: The Administrator shall, in cooperation with the Secretary of Agriculture, use the services of the cooperative State extension services to inform and educate pesticide users about accepted uses and other regulations made under this subchapter.
